title: 5. Gate 1 · Control — Ultra-Mini × TMEM145 GOLD pruned (ipTM 0.68)
lede: Derstroff et al. 2026 achieved ipTM 0.91 only by pruning TMEM145 down to its isolated GOLD domain — removing the seven transmembrane helices that collapse in solution. We reproduced that workaround on Ultra-Mini as a positive control. If the binding site is real, the pruned complex must score high. It does.
zone_sub: every single contact falls in aa 1603–1749
why1: The GOLD-pruned control collapses the ambiguity of the full-TMEM145 job. When TMEM145's 7 TM helices are removed (same protocol as the published paper), ipTM jumps from 0.43 to 0.68 and every contact residue snaps onto the canonical ARM-repeat binding zone 1603–1749. The binding surface is real; the full-length low ipTM is membrane-context noise, not an interface failure.
why2: Combined with Gate 1a (full-length TMEM145, ipTM 0.43, 23/41 in zone), this control establishes a high/low ipTM pair that brackets the real binding affinity — same pattern the Derstroff paper used to justify their published interaction claim.
